messages.content:
4) Use the words from the word bank to fill in the blanks.

digits
calculations
notations
order of magnitude
rounding error
quantity

1 )The figure was incorrect because of a(n) ….........
2) The factory produced a lower …..... than expected. 
3) A misplaced decimal makes a number one .............   off.
4) A phone number includes ten ….......
5)Jenny can do complex ….........in her head.
6) Math uses different............… to express numbers.

1) The figure was incorrect because of a(n) rounding error.
2) The factory produced a lower quantity than expected.
3) A misplaced decimal makes a number one order of magnitude off.
4) A phone number includes ten digits.
5) Jenny can do complex calculations in her head.
6) Math uses different notations to express numbers.

Match the words (1-6) with the definitions
(A-F).
1… demonstrate
4 … modify
2 … technological
5 … advertising
3… consult
6 … technical
A) related to science and technology
B) to show how something works
C )to give professional or expert advice
D)pertaining to a specific skill, art, or science
E) the process of drawing attention to products
F) to change something

1 - B) to show how something works
4 - F) to change something
2 - A) related to science and technology
5 - E) the process of drawing attention to products
3 - C) to give professional or expert advice
6 - D) pertaining to a specific skill, art, or science

Use the words from the world bank to fill in the blanks. 
commission
expertise
marketing
liaison
competitors
market research
1) …......... shows what consumers want.
2 Lower prices take business away from …..............
3 Employees get a(n)................… for signing new accounts.
4)...................… in computers is a valuable quality.
5 The ….......................… department created a new commercial.
6
Mr. Ames is a …..............… between the engineers and clients

1) Market research shows what consumers want.
2) Lower prices take business away from competitors.
3) Employees get a(n) commission for signing new accounts.
4) Expertise in computers is a valuable quality.
5) The marketing department created a new commercial.
6) Mr. Ames is a liaison between the engineers and clients.

Match the words (1-7) with the definitions(A-G).
1 … crops
5 … salinity
2 … pivot-irrigation
6 … agriculture
3… flood-irrigation
7 … localized irrigation
4 … hydrology
A covering an entire area with water
B the amount of salt in something
The science of farming
D water applied to crops in specific areas
E water applied in a circular motion to crops
F plants that are grown and sold for profit
G the study of water

1 - F) plants that are grown and sold for profit
5 - B) the amount of salt in something
2 - E) water applied in a circular motion to crops
6 - C) The science of farming
3 - A) covering an entire area with water
7 - D) water applied to crops in specific areas
4 - G) the study of water

Match the words (1-7) with the definitions(A-G).
1 … crops
5 … salinity
2 … pivot-irrigation
6 … agriculture
3… flood-irrigation
7 … localized irrigation
4 … hydrology
A covering an entire area with water
B the amount of salt in something
C The science of farming
D water applied to crops in specific areas
E water applied in a circular motion to crops
F plants that are grown and sold for profit
G the study of water

1 - F) plants that are grown and sold for profit
5 - B) the amount of salt in something
2 - E) water applied in a circular motion to crops
6 - C) The science of farming
3 - A) covering an entire area with water
7 - D) water applied to crops in specific areas
4 - G) the study of water

use the words from the world bank to fill in the blanks.
irrigation
tillage
water rights
distribution uniformity
drainage
1)............… prepares soil to support crops.
2) ............… moves water away from an area.
3).......................… assures that all crops are watered evenly.
4) A farmer's access to water depends on …......................
5) farmers set up ….......… systems to water crops.

1) Tillage prepares soil to support crops.
2) Drainage moves water away from an area.
3) Distribution uniformity assures that all crops are watered evenly.
4) A farmer's access to water depends on water rights.
5) Farmers set up irrigation systems to water crops.

Match the words (1-6) with the definitions (A-F).
1 … supply chain
4 … streamline
2 … facility layout
5 … cost-effective
3 … assembly line
6 … logistics
A the arrangement of a factory's workspace
B to make an operation more efficient
C worth the money spent
D the steps in moving a product from supplier to customer
E the organization of an operation
F a line of workers or machines in a factory

1 - D) the steps in moving a product from supplier to customer
4 - B) to make an operation more efficient
2 - A) the arrangement of a factory's workspace
5 - C) worth the money spent
3 - F) a line of workers or machines in a factory
6 - E) the organization of an operation

Choose the word that is closest in meaning to the
underlined part.
1 Designing comfortable equipment reduces injuries.
A) logistics
B) ergonomics
C) capacity
2 The equipment that moves materials is broken.
A) conveyor
B)Assembly line
C)Supply chain
3Amy managers ensuring quality and safety.
A)Ergonomics
B capacity 
C)quality control
4 Update the list of materials and goods.
A supply chain
B)conveyor
C)inventory
5 What is the production maximum amount?
A) facility layout 
B) capacity
C)quality control

1) B) ergonomics
2) A) conveyor
3) C) quality control
4) C) inventory
5) B) capacity

Match the words (1-8) with the definitions (A-H).
1… MSc
5 … EAB
2 … postgraduate degree
6 … MSc(Res)
3 … MEng
7 … undergraduate degree
4 … ABET
8 … PhD
A)   accrediting body in the United States
B)  accrediting body in the United Kingdom
C)   a master's degree in a scientific field
D)  a master's degree in a scientific field earned through research
E)  a doctorate
F)   a master's degree in engineering
G)  a degree showing four years of study
H)  any degree achieved after a bachelor's

1… C) a master's degree in a scientific field
5 … B) accrediting body in the United Kingdom (Note: EAB can represent various bodies or terminology; without more information, it is challenging to define precisely, though no widely recognized UK accrediting body uses the acronym EAB. This could be a mistake or a lesser-known entity.)
2 … H) any degree achieved after a bachelor's
6 … D) a master's degree in a scientific field earned through research
3 … F) a master's degree in engineering
7 … G) a degree showing four years of study
4 … A) accrediting body in the United States
8 … E) a doctorate
